speaker: JAQUES
text: Which is he that killed the deer?

speaker: JAQUES
text: Duke like a Roman conqueror. And it would do well

speaker: JAQUES
text: to set the deer’s horns upon his head for a branch of

speaker: JAQUES
text: victory.—Have you no song, forester, for this

speaker: JAQUES
text: Sing it. ’Tis no matter how it be in tune, so it

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: What shall he have that killed the deer?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: His leather skin and horns to wear.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Take thou no scorn to wear the horn.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: It was a crest ere thou wast born.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The horn, the horn, the lusty horn

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Is not a thing to laugh to scorn.
